{"body":"Please provide as much information as you can: 

* **Suggested term label:**
p24 cargo receptor complex (Unfortunately this is the only name I can find)

* **Definition (free text)**

A cargo receptor complex localized to the Endoplasmic Reticulum (ER) exit sites, where it helps to select cargo for packaging into COPII vesicles.

* **Reference, in format PMID:#######**

PMID:32456004
PMID:34647572
PMID:27569046

* **Gene product name and ID to be annotated to this term**

Systematic ID   Gene name       pombe Product description     Budding yeast orthologs Human orthologs
SPBC16E9.09c    erp5    COPII vesicle coat component Erp5/Erp6/Erp1     ERP1,ERP5,ERP6  TMED4,TMED9
SPCC24B10.17    emp24   COPII-coated vesicle component Emp24    EMP24   TMED2
SPAC17A5.08     erp2    COPII-coated vesicle component Erp2/3/4 ERP2,ERP3,ERP4  TMED3
SPAC23H4.03c    erv25   COPII-coated vesicle component Erv25    ERV25   TMED10

* **Parent term(s)**

GO:0062137   cargo receptor complex

* **Children terms (if applicable)** Should any existing terms that should be moved underneath this new proposed term?


* **Synonyms (please specify, EXACT, BROAD, NARROW or RELATED)**

* **Cross-references**
* For enzymes, please provide RHEA and/or EC numbers. 
* Can also provide MetaCyc, KEGG, Wikipedia, and other links.

* **Any other information**
","comments":[{"id":"IC_kwDOAkAHws7glgvw","author":{"login":"ValWood"},"authorAssociation":"MEMBER","body":"
The p24 cargo receptor complex is a conserved hetero-oligomeric complexes (often tetrameric), cycling ER–Golgi protein complex that selectively recruits secretory cargo, especially GPI-anchored proteins, into COPII vesicles and helps maintain early secretory pathway organization. A functional p24 complex typically contains one member of each subfamily of p24 proteins, alpha, beta, gamma and delta.

def synthesised form
https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/19566487/
and
https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/26224213/","createdAt":"2026-01-19T11:47:03Z","includesCreatedEdit":false,"isMinimized":false,"minimizedReason":"","reactionGroups":[],"url":"https://github.com/geneontology/go-ontology/issues/31295#issuecomment-3767929840","viewerDidAuthor":false},{"id":"IC_kwDOAkAHws7ltomT","author":{"login":"ValWood"},"authorAssociation":"MEMBER","body":"I'm not going to add this yet. ","createdAt":"2026-02-05T14:13:56Z","includesCreatedEdit":false,"isMinimized":false,"minimizedReason":"","reactionGroups":[],"url":"https://github.com/geneontology/go-ontology/issues/31295#issuecomment-3853945235","viewerDidAuthor":false},{"id":"IC_kwDOAkAHws756CHC","author":{"login":"ValWood"},"authorAssociation":"MEMBER","body":"We do actually need this because it can't be decomposed. The entire complex has "cargo receptor activity"


Anterograde (ER→Golgi): The p24 complex acts as a cargo receptor, packaging GPI-anchored proteins like Gas1p into COPII vesicles for forward transport.
(this is the role I want to annotate in http://noctua.geneontology.org/workbench/noctua-visual-pathway-editor/?model_id=gomodel%3A693b3c0900004859
I had to use "cargo receptor complex" but this is non specific, it has the child"[GO:0062136](https://www.ebi.ac.uk/QuickGO/term/GO:0062136)    low-density lipoprotein receptor complex"

p24 complex itself needs to be recycled back to the ER via COPI vesicles so it can pick up more cargo. The p24 proteins are assembled into heteromeric complexes that continuously cycle between ER and Golgi compartments. [PubMed Central](https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC2265561/)
The p24 complex also has a role in  COPI retrograde vesicle formation [PubMed Central](https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC2265561/),  p24 proteins are actively involved in generating the  vesicles that carry them back to the ER.","createdAt":"2026-04-06T14:19:49Z","includesCreatedEdit":false,"isMinimized":false,"minimizedReason":"","reactionGroups":[],"url":"https://github.com/geneontology/go-ontology/issues/31295#issuecomment-4192739778","viewerDidAuthor":false},{"id":"IC_kwDOAkAHws8AAAABBbBJwg","author":{"login":"ValWood"},"authorAssociation":"MEMBER","body":"@dragon-ai-agent  please add this complex term
","createdAt":"2026-05-06T17:18:57Z","includesCreatedEdit":false,"isMinimized":false,"minimizedReason":"","reactionGroups":[{"content":"EYES","users":{"totalCount":1}}],"url":"https://github.com/geneontology/go-ontology/issues/31295#issuecomment-4390406594","viewerDidAuthor":false},{"id":"IC_kwDOAkAHws8AAAABBbBXHw","author":{"login":"dragon-ai-agent"},"authorAssociation":"MEMBER","body":"🤖 Working on it...

Follow along: [View workflow run](https://github.com/geneontology/go-ontology/actions/runs/25450290860)

*— @dragon-ai-agent*","createdAt":"2026-05-06T17:19:28Z","includesCreatedEdit":false,"isMinimized":false,"minimizedReason":"","reactionGroups":[],"url":"https://github.com/geneontology/go-ontology/issues/31295#issuecomment-4390410015","viewerDidAuthor":true},{"id":"IC_kwDOAkAHws8AAAABBbKYrQ","author":{"login":"dragon-ai-agent"},"authorAssociation":"MEMBER","body":"Added as **GO:7770070 p24 cargo receptor complex** in PR #32040.

**Placement:** child of `GO:0062137 ! cargo receptor complex`, following the precedent of the sibling `GO:0061852 ! retrograde cargo receptor complex, Golgi to ER`. I used `capable_of_part_of GO:0006888 ! endoplasmic reticulum to Golgi vesicle-mediated transport` to capture the anterograde role, mirroring how GO:0061852 points to the retrograde Golgi→ER transport process.

**Definition** (synthesised from PMID:19566487 and PMID:26224213, with supporting cites):
> A hetero-oligomeric, typically tetrameric, cargo receptor complex that cycles between the endoplasmic reticulum (ER) and the Golgi apparatus. The complex selectively recruits secretory cargo, in particular glycosylphosphatidylinositol (GPI)-anchored proteins, into COPII-coated vesicles at ER exit sites for anterograde transport, and is itself returned to the ER via COPI-coated vesicles. A functional p24 complex typically contains one member of each of the four p24 subfamilies: alpha, beta, gamma and delta.

**Synonyms:** "p24 complex" (EXACT), "Emp24-Erv25 complex" / "p24 family complex" / "TMED complex" (RELATED).

**References used** (all PubMed-validated):
- PMID:19566487 — "The p24 family and selective transport processes at the ER-Golgi interface"
- PMID:26224213 — "p24 family proteins: key players in the regulation of trafficking along the secretory pathway"
- PMID:27569046 — "3D Structure and Interaction of p24β and p24δ Golgi Dynamics Domains: Implication for p24 Complex Formation and Cargo Transport"
- PMID:32456004 — "Dual Independent Roles of the p24 Complex in Selectivity of Secretory Cargo Export from the Endoplasmic Reticulum"
- PMID:34647572 — "Differential use of p24 family members as cargo receptors for the transport of glycosylphosphatidylinositol-anchored proteins and Wnt1"

**Notes / decisions worth flagging for review:**
- I deliberately did **not** add a logical definition (`intersection_of`). This complex isn't fully captured by "cargo receptor complex involved in ER→Golgi transport" — LMAN/ERGIC-53 also fits that frame and is a different complex. The parent's logical def already gives necessary+sufficient framing; the `capable_of_part_of` relationship narrows the role.
- I did not add a specific membrane `part_of` (e.g. ER membrane). The complex cycles through ER, ERGIC, Golgi, COPI and COPII vesicle membranes, so any single anatomical part_of would mis-localize it. The inherited `part_of GO:0016020 ! membrane` from the parent is appropriate.

Once the PR merges you should be able to use the term in your noctua model in place of the generic `cargo receptor complex`. Happy to tweak the def, synonyms, or add a separate `capable_of_part_of GO:0006890` (retrograde) link if you think the retrograde COPI role belongs on this term too — your call.

---
🤖 **Generated by @dragon-ai-agent**
- Model: `claude-opus-4-7`
- Agent harness: claude-code
- Triggered by: @ValWood
- Run: [View workflow run](https://github.com/geneontology/go-ontology/actions/runs/25450290860)","createdAt":"2026-05-06T17:42:38Z","includesCreatedEdit":false,"isMinimized":false,"minimizedReason":"","reactionGroups":[],"url":"https://github.com/geneontology/go-ontology/issues/31295#issuecomment-4390557869","viewerDidAuthor":true},{"id":"IC_kwDOAkAHws8AAAABBbRM5g","author":{"login":"ValWood"},"authorAssociation":"MEMBER","body":"@dragon-ai-agent please change 
the defintion 

A hetero-oligomeric, typically tetrameric, cargo receptor complex that cycles between the endoplasmic reticulum (ER) and the Golgi apparatus. The complex selectively recruits secretory cargo, in particular glycosylphosphatidylinositol (GPI)-anchored proteins, into COPII-coated vesicles at ER exit sites for anterograde transport, and is itself returned to the ER via COPI-coated vesicles. A functional p24 complex typically contains one member of each of the four p24 subfamilies: alpha, beta, gamma and delta.

to the provided def
The p24 cargo receptor complex is a conserved hetero-oligomeric complexes (often tetrameric), cycling ER–Golgi protein complex that selectively recruits secretory cargo, especially GPI-anchored proteins, into COPII vesicles and helps maintain early secretory pathway organization. A functional p24 complex typically contains one member of each subfamily of p24 proteins, alpha, beta, gamma and delta.

Your definition is not necessary and sufficient before the first  period, there are other cycling cargo  receptors.

Follow along: [View workflow run](https://github.com/geneontology/go-ontology/actions/runs/25452228168)

content.content:
[Term]
id: GO:7770070
name: p24 cargo receptor complex
namespace: cellular_component
def: "A hetero-oligomeric, typically tetrameric, cargo receptor complex that cycles between the endoplasmic reticulum (ER) and the Golgi apparatus. The complex selectively recruits secretory cargo, in particular glycosylphosphatidylinositol (GPI)-anchored proteins, into COPII-coated vesicles at ER exit sites for anterograde transport, and is itself returned to the ER via COPI-coated vesicles. A functional p24 complex typically contains one member of each of the four p24 subfamilies: alpha, beta, gamma and delta." [PMID:19566487, PMID:26224213, PMID:27569046, PMID:32456004, PMID:34647572]
synonym: "p24 complex" EXACT []
synonym: "Emp24-Erv25 complex" RELATED []
synonym: "p24 family complex" RELATED []
synonym: "TMED complex" RELATED []

relationship: capable_of_part_of GO:0006888 ! endoplasmic reticulum to Golgi vesicle-mediated transport
property_value: term_tracker_item "https://github.com/geneontology/go-ontology/issues/31295" xsd:anyURI

file.content:
[Term]
id: GO:7770070
name: p24 cargo receptor complex
namespace: cellular_component
def: "A hetero-oligomeric, typically tetrameric, cargo receptor complex that cycles between the endoplasmic reticulum (ER) and the Golgi apparatus. The complex selectively recruits secretory cargo, in particular glycosylphosphatidylinositol (GPI)-anchored proteins, into COPII-coated vesicles at ER exit sites for anterograde transport, and is itself returned to the ER via COPI-coated vesicles. A functional p24 complex typically contains one member of each of the four p24 subfamilies: alpha, beta, gamma and delta." [PMID:19566487, PMID:26224213, PMID:27569046, PMID:32456004, PMID:34647572]
synonym: "p24 complex" EXACT []
synonym: "Emp24-Erv25 complex" RELATED []
synonym: "p24 family complex" RELATED []
synonym: "TMED complex" RELATED []
is_a: GO:0062137 ! cargo receptor complex
relationship: capable_of_part_of GO:0006888 ! endoplasmic reticulum to Golgi vesicle-mediated transport
property_value: term_tracker_item "https://github.com/geneontology/go-ontology/issues/31295" xsd:anyURI
created_by: dragon-ai-agent
creation_date: 2026-05-06T17:36:35Z

input.command:
gh pr comment 32040 --body "$(cat <<'EOF'
Definition updated per @ValWood's request in issue comment.

**Old def:**
> A hetero-oligomeric, typically tetrameric, cargo receptor complex that cycles between the endoplasmic reticulum (ER) and the Golgi apparatus. The complex selectively recruits secretory cargo, in particular glycosylphosphatidylinositol (GPI)-anchored proteins, into COPII-coated vesicles at ER exit sites for anterograde transport, and is itself returned to the ER via COPI-coated vesicles. A functional p24 complex typically contains one member of each of the four p24 subfamilies: alpha, beta, gamma and delta.

**New def:**
> A conserved, hetero-oligomeric (often tetrameric) cycling ER-Golgi protein complex that selectively recruits secretory cargo, especially GPI-anchored proteins, into COPII vesicles and helps maintain early secretory pathway organization. A functional p24 complex typically contains one member of each subfamily of p24 proteins, alpha, beta, gamma and delta.

Reviewer's rationale: the old first sentence was not necessary-and-sufficient — other cycling cargo receptor complexes (e.g., LMAN1/MCFD2) also cycle between ER and Golgi. Curator-supplied wording incorporates the GPI-anchored cargo specificity earlier and ties in the secretory pathway organization role.

**Editorial note:** I made one minor grammar fix to the supplied wording — the original had "hetero-oligomeric complexes (often tetrameric), cycling ER–Golgi protein complex", which has the noun "complex" twice. I collapsed it to a single noun phrase: "hetero-oligomeric (often tetrameric) cycling ER-Golgi protein complex". I also used an ASCII hyphen rather than en-dash in "ER-Golgi" for consistency with the rest of go-edit.obo. Reverting either is trivial.

**Checklist:**
- [x] PLAN
- [x] PRE-VALIDATION (validated before edit)
- [N/A] RESEARCH (definition wording supplied by curator; existing PMIDs retained)
- [x] TERM-SEARCH (no other terms affected)
- [N/A] DESIGN-PATTERNS (text-only edit; no structural changes)
- [x] EDITS (via obo-checkout/obo-checkin)
- [N/A] RELATIONSHIPS (unchanged)
- [N/A] SPECIALIZED-EDITS
- [x] METADATA (definition references unchanged; created_by/creation_date unchanged — this is an edit to a not-yet-merged term but the term is technically already created)
- [x] AUTOMATED-VALIDATION — SPARQL checks pass, ELK reasoning clean
- [x] REFERENCE-VALIDATION — same five PMIDs as before, all previously validated
- [x] CHANGES-COMMITTED on PR branch

---
🤖 **Generated by @dragon-ai-agent**
- Model: \`claude-opus-4-7\`
- Agent harness: claude-code
- Triggered by: @ValWood
- Run: [View workflow run](https://github.com/geneontology/go-ontology/actions/runs/25452228168)
EOF
)"
